name: Summer Pasta Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the chopped tomatoes, shredded Brie, fresh basil, minced garlic, olive oil, and Kalamata olives. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Cover the bowl and let it sit at room temperature for 1 to 2 hours to allow the flavors to meld together.
When ready to serve:
3. Cook the linguine al dente according to package instructions. Drain and toss with the tomato sauce.
4. The heat from the pasta will melt the Brie further, creating a rich and creamy sauce.
5. Serve immediately, topped with grated Parmesan cheese, alongside a nice loaf of bread and a glass of wine.
